target_emits.name: create_essid_alias
target_emits.purpose: Dual-mode only (bridged_and_tunneled): create the ESSID alias both the bridge + tunnel profiles reference, so one logical SSID exists in two forward-modes without an essid collision. Created SHARED so it's referenceable from both scopes.

target_emits.name: create_wlan_ssid
target_emits.purpose: Single-mode (bridged / tunneled / hybrid): the one flat wlan-ssids profile merging virtual_ap (forward-mode, vlan) + ssid_prof (essid, opmode). Created LOCAL at the AP scope. forward-mode is the operator's target decision.

target_emits.name: create_wlan_bridge
target_emits.purpose: Dual-mode: the BRIDGE variant of the SSID (FORWARD_MODE_BRIDGE), referencing the ESSID alias, created LOCAL at the bridge scope (e.g. the smaller/branch sites).
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST for the bridge variant.

target_emits.name: create_wlan_tunnel
target_emits.purpose: Dual-mode: the TUNNEL variant of the SSID (FORWARD_MODE_L2), referencing the ESSID alias, created LOCAL at the tunnel scope (e.g. the large campus).
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST for the tunnel variant.

target_emits.name: create_overlay_wlan
target_emits.purpose: Single-mode tunneled/hybrid: bind the SSID to its gateway cluster(s) via overlay-wlan (literal essid-name). gw-cluster-list (which clusters + scope-ids + tunnel-type) is supplied by the skill; an SSID may bind to multiple clusters.
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST when single-mode target is tunneled / hybrid.

target_emits.name: create_overlay_wlan_dual
target_emits.purpose: Dual-mode: bind the TUNNEL variant to its gateway cluster(s) via overlay-wlan, referencing the ESSID alias (use-essid-alias=true).
target_emits.iteration_notes: One POST for the dual tunnel variant's cluster binding.

target_meta.device_function_filtering_rule: wlan-ssids + overlay-wlan are AP constructs (the SSID is broadcast/forwarded by the AP) → CAMPUS_AP. Tunneled SSIDs reach the gateway cluster via the overlay-wlan gw-cluster-list, not by being a gateway profile.

target_scope_id_resolution.rule: Central scope_id(s) supplied by the skill at runtime. Single mode: central_scope_id (the AP scope). Dual mode: bridge_scope_id + tunnel_scope_id (the per-location split for the same SSID), derived from detection / operator.
target_scope_id_resolution.input: Source ap-group binding scope + operator selection

dependencies.depends_on: central:gateway_cluster (tunneled/hybrid/dual SSIDs bind to a cluster via overlay-wlan gw-cluster-list); central:named_vlan (vlan-name references a Central named VLAN)

merge_rule.description: The engine source record is one virtual_ap. Its ssid_prof is looked up by name from runtime_values['ssid_profiles'] (the full ssid_prof list) in the aos8_wlan_ssid preprocessing, then essid/opmode are merged onto the virtual_ap before key_mappings run. Mirrors central:policy's runtime-supplied role_records join.

objects.notes: The SSID-as-broadcast. forward_mode (one-level wrapped; absent = tunnel default), vlan, ssid_prof ref, aaa_prof ref. The engine source record IS one virtual_ap.
essid + opmode + rates. Supplied via runtime_values['ssid_profiles'] and joined to the virtual_ap by ssid_prof.profile-name. opmode is {<mode-name>: true}; mapped to the Central opmode enum.

essid_alias.notes: Dual mode: the ESSID alias name (= the essid).

essid_obj_alias.notes: {use-alias: true, alias: <name>} (dual modes — both profiles share the alias).

opmode.notes: AOS 8 ssid_prof opmode key -> Central opmode enum (table in the normalizer). bSec-*/xSec are unmapped (legacy).

forward_mode.notes: Single modes only (dual variants hard-code BRIDGE / L2). bridged->FORWARD_MODE_BRIDGE, tunneled->FORWARD_MODE_L2, hybrid->FORWARD_MODE_MIXED.

emit_dual.to: (emit_when guard, alias + bridge + tunnel + dual overlay)

gw_cluster_list.notes: [{cluster, cluster-type, tunnel-type, cluster-redundancy-type, cluster-scope-id}] via runtime_values['gw_cluster_list']. An SSID may bind to multiple clusters.

unmapped_fields.from: Inline AAA on wlan-ssids (auth-server-group / acct-server-group / default-role / pre-auth-role / mac-authentication / radius-*) sourced from the virtual_ap's aaa_prof
unmapped_fields.reason: Bridge-mode SSIDs need their AAA inlined here (the AP terminates auth); tunnel-mode SSIDs lean on the gateway aaa-profile (central:aaa_profile) reached via the overlay. The full aaa_prof-join + inline AAA is deferred to a later wlan_ssid increment.

unmapped_fields.from: ssid_prof rates / wmm / he_ssid_prof / dot11k / beacon-rate; virtual_ap band-steering / dynamic-mcast-optimization / broadcast-filter / drop_mcast / wmm-traffic-management; PSK secret (wpa-passphrase / wpa-hexkey)
unmapped_fields.reason: Radio/QoS tuning + advanced VAP options deferred. The PSK secret (personal opmodes) must route through PII tokenization before it lands in any write path.

ignored_variants.reason: Consumer filters system/default VAPs at the record level before emit_calls.

draft_notes: Central wlan-ssids (forward-mode L2/MIXED/BRIDGE from the operator's target_mode) + overlay-wlan cluster binding. All 4 questionnaire modes: bridged / tunneled / hybrid (single profile, gated by emit_single + needs_overlay) and bridged_and_tunneled (essid alias + bridge profile + tunnel profile + tunnel overlay, gated by emit_dual). Created LOCAL at the AP scope via query params.
JOIN: source record = one virtual_ap; its ssid_prof is supplied via runtime_values['ssid_profiles'] and joined by name (like central:policy's role_records).

DUAL MODE (bridged_and_tunneled) requires runtime_values bridge_scope_id + tunnel_scope_id (the per-location split — e.g. large campus tunneled, branches bridged). The same SSID lands as two profiles ({name}-bridge / {name}-tunnel) sharing one ESSID alias.
TARGET MODE is the operator's decision (runtime target_mode), not the source forward_mode — detection only recommends it upstream in the skill.
DEFERRED (flagged in unmapped_fields): inline bridge-mode AAA (aaa_prof join), radio/QoS tuning, PSK secret (needs PII tokenization).
DEPENDENCY: tunneled/hybrid/dual SSIDs bind to central:gateway_cluster via overlay gw-cluster-list; vlan-name references central:named_vlan. Run those first.

NEEDS-LIVE-VERIFY (write path): device-function CAMPUS_AP vs the SSID's actual broadcast scope; opmode enum spellings for less-common modes; overlay gw-cluster-list cluster-type / tunnel-type; the SHARED essid alias scope vs a per-scope alias.
